symptoms.symptom_name: Vomiting within minutes of every milk feed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Time-locking of vomiting to milk (lactose) ingestion is the hallmark that points to a galactose/lactose metabolic disorder rather than non-specific vomiting.
Pyloric stenosis vomiting is projectile in a hungry alert baby at 3–6 weeks, not a lethargic jaundiced 6-day-old.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly progressive jaundice spreading down the body over 24 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid jaundice with hepatocellular dysfunction fits acute hepatotoxicity from accumulating galactose metabolites.
Physiological jaundice does not progress this fast or occur alongside lethargy, vomiting and hepatomegaly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound lethargy, hypotonia and weak cry (encephalopathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neonatal encephalopathy from hypoglycaemia and toxin accumulation.
Lethargy and hypotonia are classic non-specific signs of a septic neonate — mandates parallel sepsis management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jittery/shaky episode after a feed (hypoglycaemia proxy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-prandial jitteriness suggests hypoglycaemia, a recognised feature of galactosaemic crisis; patient-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor feeding and weight loss since birth

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to thrive with feeding is consistent with an evolving metabolic disorder from the first milk feeds.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-blanching petechiae on the chest (coagulopathy/sepsis marker)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Petechiae reflect hepatic coagulopathy from liver failure.
Non-blanching petechiae in an unwell neonate are a red flag for sepsis / meningococcaemia and disseminated intravascular coagulation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypothermia / cold to touch, pale and mottled (poor perfusion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neonatal sepsis characteristically causes hypothermia rather than fever, with poor perfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm hepatomegaly — liver edge 4 cm below the right costal margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hepatomegaly with hepatocellular failure — sealed clinician palpation finding, not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound hypoglycaemia — bedside capillary glucose 1.8 mmol/L

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective profound hypoglycaemia — sealed clinician bedside measurement; the AI Doctor infers hypoglycaemia from post-feed jitteriness, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distributive/septic shock — BP 65/40, HR 175, temp 35.8 C

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective shock vitals — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers shock from the caregiver's report of a cold, floppy, pale baby.
Galactitol-driven neonatal cataracts producing a dulled red reflex are highly specific for galactosaemia — sealed ophthalmoscopy finding, never obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: GALT enzyme assay / newborn-screen galactose metabolites

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deficient GALT activity is diagnostic — a hospital laboratory investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Vomiting time-locked to milk feeds + rapid jaundice + encephalopathy + hypoglycaemic jitteriness + coagulopathic petechiae in a neonate is a metabolic emergency mandating cessation of milk and immediate transfer.
Lethargy, petechiae and hypothermia in a neonate mandate treating for sepsis in parallel regardless of the metabolic cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Profound hypoglycaemia must be identified and corrected immediately — performed by the emergency team, not remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full septic screen with empirical antibiotics (blood culture, FBC, CRP, LP as able)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Any sick neonate needs sepsis work-up and empirical cover; galactosaemia predisposes to E. coli sepsis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Liver function tests and coagulation profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess hepatocellular failure and coagulopathy suggested by petechiae.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Newborn screening review / GALT enzyme assay and urine reducing substances
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive metabolic diagnosis — arranged at the paediatric centre.
